static.description: A polyglot microservices platform for food delivery, built with 5 independent services (API Gateway, Order, Inventory, Driver, Notification) across Kotlin, Java, Python, Go, and TypeScript stacks, communicating via RabbitMQ events and fronted by a JWT-authenticated API Gateway.
domain_concepts: OrderStatus: Enum representing the lifecycle states of an order: PENDING → ACCEPTED → PREPARING → READY_FOR_PICKUP → IN_TRANSIT → DELIVERED, with CANCELLED and REFUNDED as terminal/side states.
Order: JPA entity representing a customer's food order, including items, total amount, delivery address, Stripe PaymentIntent ID, and current lifecycle status.
OrderItem: Embeddable JPA entity representing a line item within an order, containing menu item ID, name, quantity, and unit price.
MenuItem: SQLAlchemy model representing a restaurant menu item with stock tracking; stock_quantity of -1 means unlimited availability.
Restaurant: SQLAlchemy model representing a restaurant with name, address, cuisine type, rating, and associated menu items.
Driver: Go struct representing a delivery driver stored in MongoDB, with name, phone, vehicle type, availability status, and GPS location.
GeoPoint: Go struct storing a driver's current GPS coordinates (latitude and longitude).
JwtAuthFilter: Spring Cloud Gateway global filter that validates JWT tokens on inbound requests and forwards the verified customer ID as X-Customer-Id header to downstream services.
order.events: RabbitMQ exchange name used for publishing and consuming order lifecycle events across services.
acquire_lock: Redis-based distributed lock acquisition function in the Inventory Service to prevent concurrent stock overselling.
OrderEvent: TypeScript interface representing an order lifecycle event consumed from RabbitMQ by the Notification Service.
isValidTransition: Static method on OrderStatus that enforces valid state machine transitions in the order lifecycle.

dynamic.architecture_summary: The system follows a microservices architecture with an API Gateway (Kotlin/Spring Cloud Gateway) performing JWT authentication and routing to downstream services. Order Service (Java/Spring Boot) handles order lifecycle and Stripe payments, publishing events to RabbitMQ which are consumed by the Notification Service (Node.js/TypeScript) for SMS/email delivery. Inventory Service (Python/FastAPI) manages restaurant menus with Redis-based distributed locks for stock reservation, and Driver Service (Go) tracks driver profiles and locations via MongoDB.

key_components.name: GatewayApplication
key_components.responsibility: Entry point for the Spring Cloud Gateway that routes and load-balances requests to downstream microservices.

key_components.name: JwtAuthFilter
key_components.responsibility: Global gateway filter that validates JWT tokens and injects the authenticated customer ID into downstream request headers.

key_components.name: OrderServiceApplication
key_components.responsibility: Spring Boot entry point for the Order Service.

key_components.name: OrderController
key_components.responsibility: REST controller handling order CRUD operations, status updates, and cancellations.

key_components.name: OrderService
key_components.responsibility: Business logic for order creation with Stripe payment integration and RabbitMQ event publishing.

key_components.name: Order
key_components.responsibility: JPA entity modeling a food delivery order with items, status, payment intent, and timestamps.

key_components.name: OrderStatus
key_components.responsibility: Enum defining the order lifecycle state machine and valid state transitions.

key_components.name: inventory-service/main.py
key_components.responsibility: FastAPI application handling restaurant/menu queries and stock reservation with Redis distributed locks.

key_components.name: inventory-service/models.py
key_components.responsibility: SQLAlchemy ORM models for Restaurant and MenuItem entities with PostgreSQL UUID primary keys.

key_components.name: driver-service/main.go
key_components.responsibility: Go HTTP service for driver profile management and location tracking backed by MongoDB.

key_components.name: notification-service/index.ts
key_components.responsibility: RabbitMQ consumer that sends SMS (Twilio) and email (SendGrid) notifications for order lifecycle events.

key_components.name: docker-compose.yml
key_components.responsibility: Orchestrates all 5 application services plus PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, and RabbitMQ infrastructure.

agent_safe_operations: Add new REST endpoints to existing controllers/routers in any service
Add new unit or integration tests
Modify business logic in OrderService, inventory main.py, or driver main.go
Add new model fields or entities (with corresponding migration awareness)
Update application configuration in application.yml, application.properties, or requirements.txt
Add new RabbitMQ event consumers or producers
Refactor internal service code without changing public API contracts
Add new notification templates in the notification service
agent_forbidden_operations: Modify or delete .env.example or .env files containing secrets
Change the openapi.yaml specification without coordinating across all services
Change database migration files or JPA/SQLAlchemy table definitions without review
Change JWT secret configuration or authentication bypass logic
Modify Stripe payment integration without thorough review
Remove or rename RabbitMQ exchange/queue names that other services depend on
Change docker-compose.yml service ports or environment variable names
potential_pitfalls: Each service uses a different language and build system — changes must respect each service's conventions (Maven for order-service, pip for inventory-service, go mod for driver-service, npm for notification-service, Gradle for api-gateway).
The Order model uses Lombok annotations (@Getter, @Setter, @Builder) — code that accesses Order fields must use generated methods, not direct field access.
Inventory service uses async SQLAlchemy (asyncpg driver) — the DATABASE_URL must use 'postgresql+asyncpg://' scheme, not plain 'postgresql://'.
OrderStatus.isValidTransition enforces a strict state machine — any new status or transition must be added to both the enum and the switch expression.
The JwtAuthFilter forwards X-Customer-Id to downstream services — all authenticated endpoints rely on this header rather than re-parsing JWT tokens.
Redis distributed locks in inventory service have a 10-second TTL — long-running stock operations could lose the lock and cause race conditions.
The notification service uses an in-memory Set for idempotency — this is not durable across restarts; production should use Redis.
Docker Compose health checks with conditions (service_healthy) mean services won't start if infrastructure containers fail — changes to health check commands can break startup order.
The Stripe integration uses MANUAL capture method — the PaymentIntent must be explicitly captured after order acceptance, not at creation time.
Multiple services share the same PostgreSQL database (fooddelivery) — schema changes in one service (e.g., orders table) could affect the other (inventory tables).
